Ad Text:
Company Description

About SEEK

SEEK’s portfolio of diverse businesses make a positive impact on a truly global scale. Our purpose is to help people live more fulfilling and productive working lives and help organisations succeed. We create world-class technology solutions to connect more people to relevant employment, education, small business and volunteer opportunities. We have a culture of high-performance in our workplaces and celebrate the diversity of our employees who contribute to the success of our organisation.

Life at SEEK

SEEK’s purpose is at the centre of everything we do. Our SEEK, which defines the way we work, is all about what makes us unique and a little bit different. Passion, Team, Delivery and Future are our principles that drive innovation and creativity. SEEK strives to support employee wellbeing by providing an amazing experience at work which led us to being named AFR BOSS Top 10 Best Places to Work in Technology (2021-2024). We are proud to work in an environment that's inclusive where everyone's unique ideas, experiences and perspectives are valued.

Our award-winning head office in Cremorne (just a 4 min walk from Richmond station) provides an exceptional space to collaborate with colleagues. The building provides sweeping views of the city, a games area, sit and stand desks at every workstation, modern end-of-trip facilities and Thursday night drinks which gives our people an opportunity to connect in a social setting.

Job Description

*12 Month FTC*

SEEK's growth ambition extends beyond core job ads. The Adjacent Revenue Development (ARD) team validates and accelerates the growth strategies that turn SEEK's acquired and adjacent businesses into scalable, repeatable revenue, before handing off to SEEK core teams to continue to grow.

The Senior Growth Manager – Adjacent Initiatives leads the commercial acceleration of Sidekicker, SEEK's flexible staffing marketplace, as it moves from Validation phase into Product Market Fit in FY27. You will own three interconnected growth plays: building a demand intelligence engine that surfaces Sidekicker at the moment of flex labour need; designing and scaling an outbound sales and marketing motion that improves CAC and ARPU against cold lead benchmarks; and scaling a SEEK Account Manager referral program that activates SEEK's deep hirer relationships as qualified pipeline.

You will report to the Head of Growth – Adjacent Initiatives, lead a squad of Growth Operations, Analytics and Growth Executives, and work cross-functionally across SEEK's Product, Marketing and Sales teams and Sidekicker's existing Growth team.
 

What You'll Be Accountable For

Validating and executing the acceleration strategy - Identify key commercial uncertainties, design experiments to resolve them quickly, and translate results into scalable growth playbooks, accountable for FY27 OKRs
Building the demand intelligence engine - Lead the discovery, design and implementation of a lead scoring model that turns data ‘signals’ into a qualified pipeline to execute against, and develop winning GTM playbooks to scale
Designing and scaling the SEEK Account Manager referral program - Design, enable and scale a referral program that qualifies SEEK hirers against Sidekicker's ICP, building a commercial motion replicable across future adjacent initiatives.
Leading your squad with commercial rigour - Direct Growth Operations, Analytics and Growth Executives to run disciplined experiments, with hypotheses, metrics and guardrails defined before you start and outcomes turned into repeatable plays.
Cross-functional orchestration - You are the threading leader that keeps SEEK Commercial, Product, and Marketing as well as Sidekicker's own teams aligned on priorities and progress, navigating the tension between moving fast in validation and building something SEEK core teams can eventually scale.
Qualifications

Essential qualifications, skills and experience

Significant experience personally owning revenue targets and delivering measurable commercial growth outcomes, you can point to specific results and explain exactly how you drove them
Extensive hands-on experience building and executing go-to-market models for products moving from validation to scale in tech, SaaS, management consulting, or a startup or new venture environment
Significant experience designing and implementing signals-based lead scoring or targeting models that turned data into a qualified, actionable pipeline
Extensive experience leading disciplined commercial experiments, defining hypotheses, success metrics and guardrails upfront, and translating results into repeatable growth playbooks
Demonstrated ability to build alignment and drive outcomes across complex, matrix or cross-functional environments where you held significant accountability without direct authority
Extensive experience presenting growth strategies and commercial recommendations to senior leadership, and facilitating structured planning sessions across diverse, cross-functional teams
Proven experience leading or managing a squad or team, directing analytical and operational resources to execute against commercial goals
Active, applied use of AI tools to accelerate growth analysis, surface hypotheses or scale experimentation

Do these behaviours/mindset expectations resonate with your skills & experience?

Commercially driven – You connect strategy to revenue outcomes, own your numbers and can point to the specific growth results you personally delivered and exactly how you drove them
Experiments-first thinker – You don't wait for perfect information, you identify the key uncertainty, design a fast experiment to resolve it and act decisively on what you learn
Analytically sharp – Equally at home in a dataset and in a room with an executive, you turn complex analysis into crisp, compelling recommendations that earn a green light
Low-ego collaborator – You build alignment through trust and sharp thinking in environments where almost everyone you need to move doesn't report to you
Clear and precise communicator – You hold a room, write recommendations that earn decisions and turn ambiguous briefs into clear plans others can execute against
Cross-functional orchestrator – You keep commercial, product and marketing teams aligned on priorities and progress, navigating the tension between moving fast and building something scalable
AI-curious and applied – You actively explore AI tools as a multiplier for growth, insight and execution — not as a shortcut, but paired with strong commercial judgement
Comfortable with ambiguity – You thrive where the playbook doesn't yet exist and are energised by the challenge of building it
Accountability mindset – You're accountable for outcomes you don't directly control and comfortable with that responsibility
Additional Information

At SEEK we offer:

Support of flexible working, including a mix of office and work from home days depending on your role.
SEEK is committed to operating sustainably and is preparing for the impacts of climate change and the transition to a low-carbon future, and is working to minimise its environmental impacts which includes a long-term emissions reduction target of net zero by FY2050
Access to a wide range of discounts on things such as health insurance, fitness, food, travel, accommodation plus many more
Frequent events including sports days, annual Christmas party, hackathon, and trivia
Free kick-start breakfast every morning and fresh fruit available all day in our offices
Casual dress – every day

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

We know teams with diverse ideas, experiences and perspectives are more creative and are critical to ensuring effective delivery and innovating to enable our future success. As such, we welcome applications from people with diverse backgrounds and life experiences, especially as they relate to gender, sexual identity, culture, faith, disability and life stages. If you have the skills, curiosity and an adaptable mindset but don't meet every responsibility or qualification listed in this advertisement, please still get in touch with us.

Should you require any specific support or adjustments throughout the recruitment process and beyond, please advise us and we will be happy to assist.

For this role, only those candidates with the eligible right to work will be considered.

SEEK kindly requests no unsolicited resumes or approaches from recruitment agencies and will not be responsible for any associated fees.

Job Title: Senior Analyst - Data, Insights and Performance | $160K - $180K inclusive
Ad Text:
Initial 6-month contract | Likely to be extended
Project focus | Enhance reporting suite using Power BI
Flexible working, 2-3 days WFH
The Opportunity

Our client is a highly regarded, Melbourne based organisation, currently undergoing an exciting period of growth and change. They offer a collaborative, supportive and flexible working environment, and deliver services that benefit the community. With a new ERP system recently in place, this is an opportunity to join a collaborative commercial finance team, where you can really have an impact.
  
You’ll use your modelling, analysis and PowerBI visualisation skills to support enhanced reporting. The role will also lead on continuous improvements and driving automation, via the use of PowerBI and data analytics tools.
  
The role will commence as an initial 6-month fixed term, though there is a strong likelihood of extension. Candidates with a notice period of two weeks or less are very welcome to apply.

The Challenge

Reporting to the Head of FP&A, key responsibilities will include:

Financial modelling
Developing Excel and Power BI templates
Extracting data from systems, transforming and uploading to create dashboards and visuals
Providing insightful analysis on key financial and non-financial data
Driving continuous improvements and automation in PowerBI; evolving and uplifting dashboards
The Expertise

Please apply for this position if you meet most of the following criteria:

Ideally you have a finance background (CPA, CA or degree); possibly you have an IT background and have since worked in Finance teams. (You must know your way around a P&L)
Additional qualifications in data analytics very highly regarded
Advanced MS Excel and modelling skills, including PowerQuery
Experience working with large datasets/databases
Knowledge of building dashboards in PowerBI
Demonstrated experience in process improvement. We don’t need a report-puller, but someone who can effect meaningful change
Very strong communication skills, willingness to show initiative to contribute to a changing, high performing team environment
The Next Step 

If this position will enhance your career and you would like further details on the client, the role, remuneration or a copy of the position description, please call Sophie Wood for a confidential discussion on 0491 237 829
Or alternatively please apply per the link below.

About the Company

Our client is a newly established health service undergoing significant growth and transformation across multiple sites in Melbourne. With a strong focus on delivering high-quality, connected care, the organisation is building its technology and digital capability from the ground up.

This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in shaping the ICT strategy and digital foundations of a modern, forward-thinking health organisation.

 

The Opportunity

This is a senior ICT leadership role, focused on designing and delivering a whole-of-organisation digital and technology strategy.

You will work closely with senior stakeholders to define a clear ICT roadmap, translate strategy into an actionable implementation plan, and bring structure to a complex, evolving environment. A key part of the role will be navigating a shared services model while ensuring the organisation can operate effectively and independently where required.

This role will suit someone who enjoys working at an enterprise level but remains close enough to delivery to drive outcomes in a practical, hands-on way.

 

What You'll Do

You will take ownership of shaping and delivering the organisation’s ICT strategy, ensuring technology enables safe, efficient and connected service delivery across a multi-site environment. This will involve balancing immediate operational challenges with longer-term strategic priorities, bringing clarity and direction to a complex and evolving landscape.

Develop and deliver a whole-of-organisation ICT strategy, including vision, principles and roadmap
Translate strategy into a structured, actionable implementation plan with clear sequencing and priorities
Assess the current ICT environment, identifying gaps, risks and opportunities for improvement
Navigate and optimise a shared services model, balancing alignment with the need for operational independence
Drive short-term stabilisation initiatives while setting up longer-term transformation
Partner closely with executive and senior stakeholders to align technology with clinical and operational needs
Oversee digital initiatives, vendor relationships and service performance to ensure outcomes are delivered
Define and shape a future-state digital operating model, including capability and workforce requirements
 

What You'll Bring

You are a senior ICT professional who operates confidently at an enterprise level, with the ability to think strategically while remaining grounded in delivery. You bring experience working within complex health or public sector environments and are comfortable navigating ambiguity, competing priorities, and multiple stakeholders.

10+ years’ experience in ICT, digital, or transformation roles,  within health  
Proven experience developing ICT strategy and translating it into practical, deliverable outcomes
Experience working in complex, multi-site environments with a mix of systems, vendors and stakeholders
Strong understanding of enterprise ICT environments, including cloud platforms, core systems and integrations
Experience working within or alongside shared service models and navigating associated challenges
Ability to engage, influence and communicate effectively with executive, clinical and operational stakeholders
Strong commercial and analytical mindset, with the ability to prioritise and make informed decisions
A pragmatic, outcomes-focused approach, with the ability to balance strategic thinking and hands-on delivery


 

Not sure if you meet these requirements? Please apply - we are happy to discuss your individual circumstances.

Brighter Futures Start Here

We are an inclusive team who power two of the most iconic and loved brands, Kmart and Target. We have more than 50,000 passionate individuals across our stores, distribution centres, and offices around the globe.

With our brands touching the lives of almost two million customers every week, you'll feel the impact of the work you'll do every day. As a leader in the retail market, we're optimistic about our future and yours. We're changing at a rapid rate, giving you challenging work that you'll be proud of.

Join the team



We are looking for a Data & Insights Business Partner to play a pivotal role in shaping how our stores operate, perform and deliver for customers. Sitting at the centre of decision‑making in Central Operations, you'll partner closely with operational and workforce leaders to turn complex data into clear, trusted and decision‑ready insights. This role is about driving outcomes - influencing priorities, optimising productivity and cost, and helping leaders make confident, evidence‑based decisions at scale.


What you'll be doing



In this role, you'll enable better, faster and more informed operational and workforce decisions by providing high quality data, analysis and insight across the store network.



You will:

Analyse complex operational and workforce data to identify trends, risks, opportunities and key performance drivers that impact productivity, cost and customer experience.
Translate technical and complex analysis into clear, decision ready insights using simple, compelling narratives that support prioritisation and action.
Design, build and maintain Power BI dashboards and reporting that provide leaders with clear visibility of performance, efficiency and cost impacts.
Extract, validate and manipulate data from multiple sources, including workforce and operational systems, ensuring accuracy, consistency and trust in all outputs.
Own data integrity, definitions and assumptions, proactively identifying and resolving data gaps, inconsistencies and risks.
Partner closely with Operational Excellence, Workforce Planning and central teams to ensure insights are fit for purpose and can be effectively translated into action.

To be successful in this role you'll have:



Demonstrated experience in a senior data, analytics or insights role within a large, complex organisation.
Strong analytical capability, with experience working across complex operational and workforce datasets.
Proven experience building decision‑ready dashboards and reporting using Power BI, with SQL or similar coding capability to extract and validate data.
The ability to translate complex analysis into clear, concise and practical insights for senior stakeholders.
A strong understanding of how data connects to operational performance, productivity and cost outcomes.
Confidence partnering with and influencing senior leaders through insight, not authority.
A commercially minded, curious and proactive approach, with the ability to operate effectively in fast‑paced environments with competing priorities.




Benefits we'll offer you:

Flexible hybrid working options through our Flex Where, Flex When, Flex Well policy.
A team member discount card that gives you a discount at Kmart, Target, Officeworks and Bunnings
Access to the Kmart for You benefits program which entitles you to exclusive discounts and benefits in the areas of entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and health - with over 60 suppliers!
We want you to feel rewarded for your commitment and hard work that's why eligible team members get the opportunity to participate in a bonus program
We offer inclusive parental leave, and ongoing support offered though our partnership with Circle In for team members of all backgrounds and family circumstances
A dynamic learning and support program
Opportunity to fast track your career through our Mentor Program as a Mentee or Mentor
A Kudos recognition program, where you are nominated by your peers to spin a wheel, to win a stack of cool prizes
Great career opportunities across the broader Wesfarmers Group


We celebrate the rich diversity of the communities in which we operate. We are committed to creating inclusive and safe environments where all our team members can contribute and succeed. We believe that all team members should feel valued, respected and safe, and strive to ensure our recruitment process is accessible and welcoming, with applications encouraged from all candidates.

We encourage you to tell us about any workplace adjustments that you would like, by emailing kmartgroupcareers@kmart.com.au.

If you would like to explore a career with Kmart and this sounds like the perfect role for you - apply now!

Working at Atlassian
Atlassians can choose where they work – whether in an office, from home, or a combination of the two. That way, Atlassians have more control over supporting their family, personal goals, and other priorities. We can hire people in any country where we have a legal entity. Interviews and onboarding are conducted virtually, a part of being a distributed-first company.

Responsibilities

Executive Engagement & Trusted Advisor: Build and maintain trusted relationships with C-suite and senior executives, ensuring Atlassian is positioned as a strategic partner in their digital and AI transformation journeys.

Outcome-Driven Success: Accelerate product adoption and deliver verifiable business outcomes, focusing on measurable value realization and expansion across global teams.

Strategic Success Planning: Lead the development and execution of Customer Success Plans (CSPs) that align with customer business goals, including regular check-ins, QBRs, and strategic planning sessions. Leverage CSPs as a vehicle for driving adoption, retention, and expansion.

Complex Program Leadership: Guide customers and internal teams through complex implementation and transformation initiatives, often involving multiple partners and stakeholders.

AI & Emerging Tech Enablement: Advise customers on leveraging Atlassian’s AI and emerging technologies to enhance their strategies and operations.

Risk Mitigation: Proactively identify risks, escalate issues, and drive mitigation strategies in partnership with account teams.

Voice of the Customer: Serve as the customer advocate internally, providing actionable feedback to improve Atlassian’s products, services, and customer experience.

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with sales, product, support, and advisory services to deliver a seamless customer experience and maximize business value.

Qualifications

10+ years in Customer Success, account management, consulting, or enterprise transformation, with a proven track record managing the largest, most complex strategic customers.

Executive presence and experience delivering solutions at the SVP/C-level for Fortune 500/global enterprise customers.

Demonstrated success in leading transformational initiatives that drive measurable customer outcomes (e.g., differentiated use cases, efficiency savings, accelerated growth and business outcomes (e.g., platform adoption, expansion, retention).

Experience with AI and digital transformation in enterprise environments.

Strong understanding of Jira, Confluence, and Atlassian solution use cases, with the ability to advise on solution trade-offs and best practices.

Proficiency with Customer Success Planning frameworks; experience using Gainsight, Salesforce, Tableau, or similar tools.

Excellent problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills.

Preferred: 5+ years with Atlassian-powered solutions, ITSM certification, experience with MEDDPICC or similar deal qualification frameworks.

Atlassian offers a wide range of perks and benefits designed to support you, your family and to help you engage with your local community. Our offerings include health and wellbeing resources, paid volunteer days, and so much more. To learn more, visit go.atlassian.com/perksandbenefits.

About Atlassian

At Atlassian, we're motivated by a common goal: to unleash the potential of every team. Our software products help teams all over the planet and our solutions are designed for all types of work. Team collaboration through our tools makes what may be impossible alone, possible together.

We believe that the unique contributions of all Atlassians create our success. To ensure that our products and culture continue to incorporate everyone's perspectives and experience, we never discriminate based on race, religion, national origin, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, or marital, veteran, or disability status. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

To provide you the best experience, we can support with accommodations or adjustments at any stage of the recruitment process. Simply inform our Recruitment team during your conversation with them.

To learn more about our culture and hiring process, visit go.atlassian.com/crh.

Join the team



We have two opportunities for Workforce Planning Analysts to join our Central Operations team, supporting the delivery of accurate, compliant and executable workforce plans across our store network.



Working closely with Store Operations, People & Capability, Finance, Tech and Workforce teams, you'll play a key role in translating operational demand, labour standards and budgets into practical workforce plans that enable effective store execution and productivity improvement.


What you'll be doing



In these roles, you'll deliver high quality workforce planning analysis and insights that support efficient workforce deployment and proactive wages management across stores.



Key responsibilities include:



Develop and maintain workforce planning analysis that translates operational demand, workflow inputs, labour standards and budgets into executable workforce plans and roster inputs.
Support the execution and optimisation of store rosters, including adherence to model rosters and governance processes for roster changes and variations.
Analyse labour standards, workload drivers and store operating rhythms to ensure workforce models are practical, compliant and fit for execution.
Provide technical expertise across load based scheduling, wages time of day optimisation and AI enabled smarter scheduling, including use of platforms such as UKG Pro.
Deliver clear, actionable workforce insights that identify trends, risks and opportunities related to labour deployment, productivity and efficiency.
Maintain and enhance workforce planning data sources, dashboards and reporting (including Power BI) to ensure accuracy, consistency and usability.
Partner with stakeholders across operations, workforce, finance and technology to support workforce planning initiatives and continuous improvement.

To be successful in this role you'll have:



Proven experience in workforce planning, rostering, labour analytics or operational planning in a large, complex environment.
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to turn complex data into clear, practical and actionable insights.
Experience working with workforce planning or scheduling systems, with exposure to load‑based or AI‑enabled scheduling tools preferred.
A solid understanding of awards, agreements and conditions that inform compliant and optimised workforce planning decisions.
Strong attention to detail, with the ability to document assumptions, rules and decisions to support governance and auditability.
Confidence working as a technical subject matter expert and influencing outcomes without formal authority.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across functions and operational teams.
Comfort operating in fast‑moving environments and responding to operational change while maintaining planning integrity.
A proactive, curious mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and delivering tangible productivity outcomes.

Ad Text:
We're committed to Finding Better Ways™ and that starts with creating a safe, inclusive, and empowering environment for everyone.
Exciting opportunity to be involved with a cutting-edge technology uplift for a global enterprise company
Role can be based out of our Perth or Brisbane Hub

We're Finding Better Ways™ to provide materials the world needs, now and in the future. Our values - care, courage and curiosity - guide how we work and how we treat each other.

About the role

We are seeking Technical Business Analysts (Reporting & Analytics) to support the uplift, integration and decommissioning of legacy reporting solutions as part of Rio Tinto's Modern ERP and data transformation journey. These roles play a critical part in transitioning reporting from ECC through BW/4HANA and into emerging analytics platforms, enabling standardised, sustainable and business‑aligned reporting outcomes.

Working closely with technical delivery teams and business stakeholders, you will lead analysis, impact assessment and requirements definition across reporting and analytics initiatives. The focus is on ensuring business priorities are clearly understood, risks are managed, and reporting outcomes are aligned to enterprise standards, architecture and long‑term strategy.

About you

Strong experience as a Technical or Reporting Business Analyst, supporting reporting and analytics initiatives within large, complex organisations
Solid technical understanding of SAP reporting and data, with experience across platforms such as ECC, S/4HANA, BW/4HANA or related analytics solutions
Proven ability to translate complex business requirements into clear, structured reporting and analytics outcomes, working closely with technical delivery teams
Experience performing impact assessments, existing report analysis, test planning, defect management and UAT coordination
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to work effectively across business, technology and delivery teams
A focus on standardisation, governance and quality assurance, with an eye toward scalable, industrialised reporting solutions

What we offer

Be recognised for your contribution, your thinking and your hard work, and go home knowing you've helped the world progress
Attractive compensation that is aligned to the specialist nature and impact of the role
Flexible working arrangements
A safety-focused and inclusive working environment
Career development to further your ambitions
Ongoing individual wellbeing support
Flexible working arrangements
Be part of Rio Tinto's global Modern ERP transformation, contributing directly to one of the organisation's most significant multi‑year programs
High‑impact contract role where your expertise helps shape the future of Planning & Reporting across a global enterprise

Where you'll be working

You'll join the Modern ERP Program - a multi‑disciplinary team bringing together technology, process, data and change specialists to reshape how work happens across Rio Tinto. We're modernising a 20‑year‑old ERP system and redesigning core business processes to improve safety, efficiency and ignite innovation.

This work supports our people across global operations to spend less time on transactional tasks and more time on work that really matters. By simplifying and digitising how the organisation runs, the Modern ERP Program is creating the foundation for future productivity gains, smarter decision‑making and AI‑enabled transformation.

#gts

About Rio Tinto

Rio Tinto is a leading global mining and materials company. We operate in 35 countries where we produce iron ore, copper, aluminium, critical minerals, and other materials needed for the global energy transition and for people, communities, and nations to thrive.

We have been mining for 150 years and operate with knowledge built up across generations and continents. Our purpose is finding better ways to provide the materials the world needs - striving for innovation and continuous improvement to produce materials with low emissions and to the right environmental, social and governance standards. But we can't do it on our own, so we're focused on creating partnerships to solve problems, create win-win situations and meet opportunities.

Respect and Inclusion

At Rio Tinto, we particularly welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, women, the LGBTI+ community, mature workers, people with disabilities and people from different cultural backgrounds.

We are committed to an inclusive environment where people feel comfortable to be themselves. We want our people to feel that all voices are heard, all cultures respected and that a variety of perspectives are not only welcome - they are essential to our success. We treat each other fairly and with dignity regardless of race, gender, nationality, ethnic origin, religion, age, sexual orientation or anything else that makes us different.

Salary: Benefits designed to make life better
Ad Text:
We're committed to Finding Better Ways™ and that starts with creating a safe, inclusive, and empowering environment for everyone.
Exciting opportunity to be involved with a cutting-edge technology uplift for a global enterprise company
Role can be based out of our Perth or Brisbane Hub

We're Finding Better Ways™ to provide materials the world needs, now and in the future. Our values - care, courage and curiosity - guide how we work and how we treat each other.


About the role


We are seeking a Power BI Consultant with demonstrated experience working across SAP data and Power BI reporting in a large enterprise environment.

This is an excellent opportunity for a solutions‑focused professional who enjoys tackling complex challenges and has an end‑to‑end understanding of the reporting landscape - from SAP source data and data models through to secure, performant Power BI dashboards.

About you

A strong commitment to your own safety and the safety of your team

Experience in mortgage lending, banking, consumer finance.

Hands-on experience with scorecard development, logistic regression, and model validation.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.

Ability to explain technical concepts to business stakeholders.

Preferred Experience

Mortgage origination and servicing data

WOE/IV, segmentation, and model monitoring

Responsible lending and consumer credit (NCCP) compliance considerations

Model documentation, validation support, and governance processes

About the Role

Reporting into the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ), this role is pivotal in connecting data-driven insights with commercial decision-making across the ANZ Go-to-Market (GTM) function. You will translate complex analysis into clear recommendations that shape strategy, enable operational efficiency and support execution at scale.

Working cross-functionally with key ANZ stakeholders — including Sales and Customer Success leadership — you will develop a deep understanding of the strategic, operational and financial drivers of the Australian business, research and analyse emerging trends and performance outcomes, identify improvement opportunities, and take a hands-on approach to validating and implementing solutions.

The focus of the role is to strengthen the analytical foundation of the ANZ GTM function, improving visibility of revenue performance drivers, enhancing performance reporting, and where applicable, supporting the wider Global Commercial Finance and RevOps teams in refining GTM systems and processes. You will play a critical role in shaping the operational landscape, supporting month-end insights, and ensuring our ANZ GTM teams are empowered with timely, accurate and actionable data.

What You'll Do

Revenue Reporting, Forecasting and Analysis

Attend and contribute to weekly ANZ forecast calls with Sales and Customer Success leaders, interpreting and sense-checking pipeline and revenue numbers.
Contribute to the weekly Flash Report and other regular reporting cycles.
Understand and interpret key ANZ metrics including new bookings, churn, downgrade, retention and price increase performance.
Together with the data team, design and maintain dashboards and visual reporting templates to surface financial performance trends, patterns and insights to support ANZ GTM leadership decision-making.
Partner with senior management to enable data-driven decisions, identifying data requirements across market analysis, planning, monitoring, financial modelling, pricing and reporting.
Support the Director, GTM Finance (ANZ) by contributing to budgeting, forecasting and reporting for consolidation.

Commission & Month-End Support

Provide ANZ-specific context and input into commission calculations and month-end processes, partnering with the global Commercial Finance team to ensure accuracy and local relevance.
Interpret ANZ-specific commission structures and edge cases, flagging issues and opportunities as they arise.

Strategic Analysis

Deliver analysis and insights on key strategic issues on an ad-hoc basis, including market intelligence, competitive landscape, product and pricing optimisation and growth opportunities.
Partner with GTM, Product and Finance teams to support strategic planning, investment decisions and commercial growth initiatives.
Build business cases and scenario models to assess new markets, partnerships and GTM strategies.

Stakeholder Engagement & Reporting

Own key stakeholder relationships with ANZ GTM leadership, supporting business case development and KPI reporting.
Deliver insights and recommendations that inform strategic and operational decision-making across the ANZ GTM organisation.

Process Evaluation & Improvement

Working alongside the RevOps team, assess, evaluate, and optimise business processes, anticipating requirements, identifying gaps and developing solutions that drive efficiency, accuracy and scalability.

Global Collaboration

Build and maintain strong relationships with global business partners, ensuring consistent understanding of data flows, inputs and outputs from multiple systems.
Act as a bridge between ANZ and global teams to standardise reporting and analytical processes.
Mentor and coach junior staff members within the team where applicable.

Qualifications


Experience

5+ years' experience in commercial finance, business analytics or commercial operations — ideally within a SaaS or technology business.
Go-to-market or commercial finance experience is highly regarded — familiarity with pipeline, renewals, commissions, bookings and churn metrics is a strong advantage.

Skills

Strong commercial acumen with deep analytical skills.
Lateral and critical thinking; ability to self-start and execute on non-process driven outcomes.
Advanced Excel skills with the ability to build and manage complex financial models.
Proven ability to influence decision-makers and present data-driven insights with clarity and impact.
Experience in revenue forecasting, financial analysis, and a solid understanding of KPIs and commercial drivers.

Growth-oriented mindset with a drive to continuously improve and adapt.

Stepping into the heart of the NSW Government

When you join NSW Treasury as the Director, Intergenerational Reporting, you will be empowered to lead a high-performing team within the Macroeconomy Division.  You will play a critical role influencing from the centre of government in NSW.

You will be key to leading long-term forecasting of NSW’s fiscal position and key macroeconomic aggregates.         

You’ll be given opportunities to further enhance and develop your skills by joining senior executive leadership training programs and being part of challenging projects.

We consider work-life balance a key feature of life at Treasury. You will have a high degree of autonomy to structure your work schedule around times to suit both your life outside of the workplace and in line with deliverables.

Enrich your career with NSW Treasury as Director, Intergenerational Reporting, Macroeconomy Division

As Director, Intergenerational Reporting, you will lead a specialist branch responsible for delivering long‑term economic and fiscal analysis that informs Government decisions affecting future generations. You will oversee the development of Treasury’s long‑term fiscal pressures model and the NSW Intergenerational Report, providing rigorous, trusted insights into the State’s long‑term opportunities, risks and structural challenges.

Reporting line

Reporting to the Executive Director, Macroeconomy Division, you will work closely with senior executives to support the Treasurer, Secretary and Government with authoritative advice on long‑term economic, fiscal and demographic issues.

Operating environment

This role operates in a complex and high‑profile policy environment, requiring strong judgement, resilience and the ability to manage ambiguity. You will lead major analytical programs under tight timeframes, often with incomplete information, while balancing competing priorities and sensitive stakeholder interests.

What you’ll do (and why it matters)

Lead long‑term analysis: Oversee economic modelling, research and projections underpinning the NSW Intergenerational Report and long‑term fiscal reporting.

Influence Government decisions: Provide strategic, evidence‑based advice on long‑term economic and fiscal sustainability.

Deliver major publications: Lead the delivery and ongoing development of the NSW Intergenerational Report as a trusted, enduring policy tool.

Engage with credibility: Represent Treasury across government, academia and expert forums on complex, long‑term policy issues.

Build capability: Lead and develop a high‑performing technical team delivering rigorous and impactful analysis.

How you will create a positive impact for the people of NSW

Policy leadership: Shape Treasury’s advice on long‑term economic, fiscal and demographic challenges facing NSW.

Rigorous modelling: Maintain and enhance long‑term fiscal and economic models to support high‑quality advice.

Trusted insights: Provide authoritative analysis on emerging risks, opportunities and structural pressures.

Collaboration and influence: Work across agencies to build a whole‑of‑government understanding of intergenerational issues.

People leadership: Lead, support and develop staff to deliver high‑quality outcomes with resilience and integrity.

You will join our Macroeconomy Division and will be working within the Economic Strategy and Productivity Group. Read more about your future Group and Division.

Investing in you

We value our employees, and this commitment is reflected in the development we offer.

Our Leading at Treasury curriculum comprises leadership programs to improve your effectiveness at every stage of your career. Through a mix of self-paced, online learning, in-person workshops and formal programs, you will learn how to lead, influence and engage others.

Master Data Specialist

Contract: 6-Months with the view to be extended further
Location: Brisbane or Adelaide (flexible / hybrid arrangements considered)
Industry: Mining

About the Role
Our client is seeking an experienced Master Data Specialist to support a major data and analytics uplift across asset‑intensive operations. This contract role will play a critical part in improving data quality, governance, and reliability outcomes, with a strong focus on Snowflake and Snowflake AI capabilities.

You will work closely with reliability, asset management, operations, and analytics teams to ensure master data foundations support advanced analytics, AI‑driven insights, and operational decision‑making across the business.

Key Responsibilities
Own and manage master data domains including asset, maintenance, equipment, materials, and finance data within an asset‑heavy mining environment.
Drive data quality, governance, and standardisation, ensuring master data is accurate, complete, and fit for advanced analytics and AI use cases.
Work hands‑on with Snowflake, including Snowflake AI features, to support data modelling, optimisation, and intelligent data pipelines.
Partner with Reliability and Asset Management teams to align master data structures with maintenance strategies, performance reporting, and lifecycle management.
Support data migration, cleansing, and harmonisation initiatives from legacy systems into Snowflake.
Collaborate with Data Engineers, Data Scientists, and BI teams to enable end‑to‑end analytics solutions.
Apply strong change management and data stewardship practices to embed sustainable data standards across the organisation.
About You
Proven experience as a Master Data Specialist / Master Data Analyst / Data Steward within mining, resources, or other asset‑intensive industries.
Strong understanding of asset, maintenance, reliability, and operational data (e.g. equipment hierarchies, work management, materials).
Hands‑on experience with Snowflake, with Snowflake AI exposure highly desirable.
Solid SQL capability and experience supporting analytics and reporting platforms (e.g. Power BI).
Demonstrated experience in data governance, data quality frameworks, and master data lifecycle management.
Ability to work effectively across technical and operational stakeholders, translating business needs into robust data solutions.
Experience with SAP (PM/MM) or similar ERP systems is highly advantageous.

Lead a high-impact digital portfolio shaping the future of Queensland Rail
Embed best-practice to drive performance and accountability
Trusted advisor on digital investment, delivery risk, and strategic outcomes

About Queensland Rail

Queensland Rail has been connecting communities since 1865 while supporting local jobs, industries, and economies. Queensland Rail respectfully acknowledges the Traditional Owners and Elders of the lands and waterways on which their network sits, and on those lands and waterways for over 150 years, Queensland Rail's teams have built a network that connects communities.

Our 8,000-strong workforce delivers travel, tourism and freight services across Queensland Rail's South East Queensland (SEQ) and Regional networks, and is committed to providing world-class rail services for customers that are safe and reliable. With total assets over $9 billion and a network that extends more than 6,600 kilometres across the state, the organisation aims to deliver efficient and sustainable services that create value for customers and Queenslanders.

Queensland Rail has an incredibly bright and exciting future ahead. Our purpose is to connect communities and our vision is to deliver world-class rail services for our customers. In the lead up to 2032, Queensland Rail will integrate more than $20 billion of new assets, delivering more trains, new and upgraded stations, and, ultimately, better services for our customers.

About the Role

Reporting to the Group Executive Digital and Information, the Group Senior Manager, Digital Delivery provides strategic leadership and oversight of Queensland Rail's enterprise digital portfolio. You will drive the successful delivery of business-led, technology-led, and externally delivered initiatives, shaping digital outcomes that enhance operational efficiency, improve customer experience, and support organisational transformation.

Bringing a whole-of-enterprise perspective, you will translate strategic priorities into measurable outcomes. You will lead and continuously improve portfolio, program, and project management practices, alongside solution architecture governance.

Success in this role will involve building strong partnerships across the business, embedding a commercially focused mindset, and leading a high-performing team of portfolio, program, and project professionals. You will foster a culture of accountability, agility, and customer focus while ensuring consistent and effective delivery across the digital portfolio.

About You

You are an experienced senior leader with a proven track record in delivering complex digital portfolios within large-scale environments. You bring strong defensive and strategic leadership in the face of rapidly evolving technology and threat landscapes, with an appreciation for risks associated with AI-driven capabilities, sophisticated cyber threats, third-party ecosystems, and emerging technology challenges.

You bring deep expertise in portfolio governance frameworks, risk management, and solution architecture, along with demonstrated success in leading and embedding sustainable change. You are experienced in modern digital delivery practices, including product-centric delivery, agile at enterprise scale, and platform-based architecture, and understand how to leverage Artificial Intelligence to accelerate initiatives and deliver improved business outcomes.

You possess well-developed commercial acumen, including experience managing third-party vendors within outcomes-based delivery models, with a strong awareness of associated risk and governance considerations. Your ability to engage, influence, and build trusted relationships with executive stakeholders is a key strength.

You are also recognised for your ability to lead and develop multidisciplinary teams, driving performance through clarity, transparency, and the effective use of data and insights.

The Benefits

This is a permanent, enterprise-wide leadership opportunity offering a competitive remuneration package and a Brisbane CBD location. The role provides the opportunity to shape digital outcomes that have a meaningful impact on communities across Queensland.

You will join a collaborative and values-driven organisation that supports its people to perform at their best. Queensland Rail is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where everyone can thrive.

Group Executive Manager – Technology
A Strategic Technology Leadership Opportunity

Aptella is a recognised leader in intelligent positioning and automation solutions, partnering with customers to deliver innovation, precision, and measurable outcomes across complex industries.

We are seeking a commercially astute and strategically focused technology executive to join the Executive Leadership Team, reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer. This role can be based in either Bundoora VIC or Silverwater NSW.

This is a unique opportunity to shape the future direction of technology, data, and digital capability within a growing and ambitious business.

The Role

The Group Executive Manager – Technology is accountable for defining and executing a cohesive technology strategy that enables growth, strengthens operational performance, and enhances customer value.

Operating at the intersection of CIO and CTO, the role spans enterprise technology, digital transformation, and innovation.

Key accountabilities include:

Setting and delivering a forward-looking Group Technology Strategy aligned to business objectives

Leading enterprise-wide digital transformation, including data, analytics, and AI initiatives

Ensuring technology platforms are secure, scalable, and aligned with business processes

Establishing robust governance across architecture, cybersecurity, and data management

Overseeing key enterprise systems, including Microsoft Dynamics 365

Partnering with business leaders to enable innovation in customer-facing solutions

As a trusted advisor to the CEO and Executive, you will play a central role in shaping strategy and driving organisational performance.

The Individual

You are an experienced technology executive with a track record of delivering enterprise-wide transformation and aligning technology with commercial outcomes.

You bring:

Experience in a senior technology leadership role (CIO, CTO or equivalent) within a complex environment

Demonstrated success in strategy development, transformation, and execution

Deep expertise across enterprise systems, cybersecurity, cloud, and data

Experience leading digital and AI-enabled initiatives that deliver tangible business value

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at Executive and Board level

A balanced, pragmatic leadership style with a focus on outcomes, people, and performance

The Opportunity

This is a rare opportunity to join a high-performing leadership team and take ownership of a critical function during a period of growth and transformation.

You will be empowered to shape and lead the technology vision of the organisation, drive meaningful innovation and digital advancement, build capability across the business, and deliver measurable impact for both customers and the organisation.

Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) is a leading provider of information technology, consulting, and business process services, dedicated to helping the world's leading companies build stronger businesses. Job Title: Senior Project Officer - Research Analysis, Data and Reporting
Ad Text:
The Opportunity

The Senior Project Officer will play a key role in supporting the delivery of UNSW’s strategy contributing to the development of simple, effective and trust-based systems, aligning and streamlining internal processes to allow researchers to focus on high-impact work.

This project role is hands-on rather than technical delivery. You’ll be responsible for planning, communications and documentation across a portfolio of concurrent initiatives (typically 10–20), ensuring stakeholders remain informed, aligned and on track. The role will proactively identify risks, overlaps and opportunities for greater efficiency across projects within a large, complex organisational environment.

You will work closely with a broad range of stakeholders to support project governance, maintain clear and consistent communications, and bring structure to complex and evolving initiatives. This includes preparing high-quality reports, briefings and presentations, and ensuring project documentation is accurate and up to date.

The role requires a confident, proactive approach, with the ability to ask questions and challenge where appropriate and bring clarity to ambiguity. You will be naturally curious and analytical, able to critically assess information and connect insights across multiple streams of work. 

About UNSW:

UNSW isn’t like other places you’ve worked. We are a large organisation with a diverse and highly talented community doing extraordinary work. We are driven to be thoughtful, practical and purposeful in all we do, and this collective approach is what gives our work impact. It is also why UNSW is consistently ranked among the world’s top 20 universities in the QS World University Rankings and is a proud member of Australia’s prestigious Group of Eight. If you’re seeking a career where you can thrive, be challenged and contribute to meaningful work, you’re in the right place.

Benefits and Culture: 

UNSW offer a competitive salary and access to benefits including:

17% superannuation and annual leave loading
Hybrid/Flexible working
Discounts and entitlements  
Affordable on-campus parking 

Who you are

You can demonstrate your experience in project management including the project life cycle and development of project plans, objectives and documentation.

You have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a high level of attention to detail for deliverables produced, and success working effectively and collaboratively on initiatives with a range of people at different levels within an organisation but also to work independently.

Strong capability in Excel, SharePoint, and PowerPoint is essential, as well as time management skills to respond to changing priorities, manage multiple tasks and meet competing deadlines by using judgement and initiative.

You’ll also be able to demonstrate your commitment to UNSW’s aims, objectives and values in action, together with relevant policies and guidelines, as well as health & safety (psychosocial and physical) responsibilities.

 To Apply: Make each day matter with a meaningful career at UNSW. Submit your application online before 11:55pm, Tuesday 28th May 2026. A copy of the Position Description can be found on JOB@UNSW.

Pre-Employment Checks 
Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.  

These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:-   

National and International Criminal history checks 
Entitlement to work and ID checks 
Working With Children Checks  
Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration  
Verification of relevant qualifications 
Verification of relevant professional membership  
Employment history and reference checks 
Financial responsibility assessments/checks. 
Medical Checks and Assessments 

Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW. 

Contact: Shiree Thomas, Talent Acquisition Partner e: shiree.thomas@unsw.edu.au

Please apply through the application portal and not via the contact above. 

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff.

The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment.

Solve by Talent is partnering with the University of Sydney to recruit a Senior ICT Business Partner (Research) to support the delivery of key Information and Communications Technology program (ICT) initiatives that help shape the future of education, research, and innovation.  
  
The Research function enables world-class discovery by partnering with research teams to deliver the digital tools, platforms, and services that support the full research lifecycle. From improving access to data and systems to enhancing collaboration, compliance, and research outcomes, this work helps strengthen the University’s global research impact. 
  
Why join the University of Sydney? 
  
Join one of Australia’s leading universities at a pivotal point of change, where technology is evolving to better support student learning, empower teaching excellence, and enable research delivery at global scale. 
  
ICT is central to delivering the University’s academic mission. Working across faculties, disciplines, and research communities, you will shape the platforms, services, and partnerships that enable student success, strengthen teaching, and accelerate research impact. 
  
This work spans both the critical services that keep the University running every day and the ongoing improvement of systems and experiences. 
  
With strong investment and clear direction, you’ll operate in a large and complex environment where your contribution has visible, meaningful impact across the University. 
  
You’ll collaborate across academic and professional communities to help shape a more connected and capable University, supporting teaching excellence, research impact, and student success. 
  
The opportunity 
  
As Senior ICT Business Partner, you will act as a key strategic interface between ICT and the broader University, building trusted relationships with senior stakeholders and helping align technology services, solutions and initiatives with business priorities.  
  
You will bring together stakeholder engagement, planning, delivery oversight and service improvement, helping shape demand, translate needs into actionable requirements, and ensure ICT delivers value in a way that is practical, collaborative and outcome focused.  
  
What you’ll do 

Partner with senior stakeholders to understand priorities, challenges and future needs, and align ICT responses accordingly  
Translate business needs into high-level ICT requirements, roadmap inputs, business cases and delivery priorities  
Provide oversight across initiatives and services, helping manage risk, expectations, progress and outcomes  
Support demand planning, service improvement and effective resource use across a complex operating environment  
Act as a trusted point of contact between business areas and ICT, influencing decisions and strengthening engagement  
What we’re looking for 

Proven experience in a senior business partnering, relationship management, or similar role within a university environment, supporting research portfolios, faculties, or research-intensive functions  
Strong understanding of the research landscape in higher education, including the needs of researchers, research support teams, and the systems and services that enable research delivery  
Demonstrated ability to partner with senior academic and professional stakeholders to shape priorities, influence decisions, and drive meaningful outcomes  
Experience working in complex institutional environments, navigating governance, competing priorities, and cross-functional stakeholder groups  
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to translate research and business needs into strategic technology opportunities 
What’s in it for you? 
  
At the University of Sydney, we support our staff with a range of benefits designed to enhance your career, wellbeing and work-life balance, including: 

Up to 17% superannuation to support your financial future 
Extremely generous parental leave entitlements. 
Competitive salary with salary packaging options 
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements 
Career development and learning opportunities 
Health and wellbeing programs including Fitness Passport 
Access to a vibrant, globally recognised university community 
How to apply 
  
If you’re looking for an opportunity to contribute to meaningful ICT initiatives while building strong strategic partnerships in a world-class university environment, we would love to hear from you. 

Accessibility and inclusion 
  
The University of Sydney is committed to creating an inclusive recruitment experience for all candidates. If you require adjustments or additional support at any stage of the recruitment process, we encourage you to let us know. This may include adjustments to the interview format, additional time, breaks, accessible interview locations, receiving materials in advance, or any other practical support that will help you participate comfortably. 
  
Pre-employment checks 
  
Your employment is conditional upon the completion of all role required pre-employment or background checks in terms satisfactory to the University. Similarly, your ongoing employment is conditional upon the satisfactory maintenance of all relevant clearances and background check requirements. If you do not meet these conditions, the University may take any necessary step, including the termination of your employment. 
  
Also, to meet the University’s obligations under the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Eliminate Gender-Based Violence you will be asked to declare if you have been investigated for, or found to engaged in, sexual harm or gender-based violence in the course of previous employment or in a legal process. 
  
Sponsorship / work rights for Australia 
  
You must have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this employment to be eligible to apply. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment.

Job Title: Product Manager - Global Share Plans & Tax
Ad Text:

Combining proprietary cloud-native technology with expert service, Automic simplifies investor administration and governance for companies and funds. It provides the market's leading registry, employee share plans, IR analytics, and fund administration services for over 1,400 ASX-listed and unlisted organisations. Opportunity to grow with the business

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information into concise, compelling portfolio narratives.

Relevant qualifications or certification in business, data analytics, or project management, with experience in regulated, financial, or member‑based organisations viewed favourably.

Job Title: FP&A Reporting Manager - Transformation Focus - 12 Month FTC
Ad Text:
Our client is an established ASX-listed consumer products company with a strong portfolio of recognisable brands. Finance is undergoing a period of deliberate transformation, and this fixed term hire is central to that agenda.

About the Role

This role is a 12 Month FTC with a view to go permanent. Reporting to the Head of FP&A, the FP&A Reporting Manager will contribute to core group planning and reporting activities, while directing the majority of their focus toward transformation. In the near term that means improving the tools, models, and processes that underpin FP&A — driver-based forecasting, reporting automation, planning system enhancements, and better data visibility across the group.

Demonstrated experience implementing FP&A tools and processes — forecasting models, reporting automation, planning system enhancements
Hands-on experience with planning and BI platforms such as TM1, Anaplan, Power BI, or equivalent is ideal
Exposure to ERP environments and how finance data flows through integrated systems — prior ERP implementation experience preferred but not essential.
A genuine process improvement mindset with the confidence to challenge how things are currently done
Clear communicator who can work effectively across finance and with IT and project stakeholders

A role with genuine scope beyond the day-to-day FP&A cycle
Clear pathway onto a high-visibility ERP project within six months
A business actively investing in its finance function
Competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience

About the Role

We're looking for a highly skilled and experienced Senior SAP Functional Analyst to join our dynamic IT team at our Daikin Park in Warwick Farm, NSW. In this role, you will analyse the business problems and requirements to design a fully functional solution, assisting the Enterprise Architect to evaluate and finalise the SAP solutions. This is a fantastic opportunity to make a significant impact within a leading global company.

Your key responsibilities will include:


SAP System Expertise & Support: Leading the entire lifecycle of SAP Supply Chain Management (SCM) module (including WM, MM, PP) implementation and optimisation. You'll translate business needs into effective SAP solutions to enhance the supply chain, improve inventory, and optimise production, ensuring seamless integration with other SAP systems and providing functional expertise throughout project phases.

Business Process Analysis and Optimisation: Focuses on optimising supply chain management processes by analysing current operations and recommending improvements. You'll collaborate closely with business users to gather and document their requirements, then map these needs to existing or new SAP functionalities. A key objective is to identify and implement process automation and efficiency gains to streamline operations and reduce costs.


SAP Configuration and Customisation: Involved in the configuration of SAP modules like SCM (Core WM, MM and PP) to align with business requirements and best practices. You'll also develop functional specifications for custom developments, collaborating closely with the technical team. A key responsibility is to conduct and support testing phases (unit, integration, and UAT) to ensure SAP solutions function correctly and perform optimally.

Problem Resolution & Optimisation: Act as a key resource for troubleshooting and resolving critical SAP incidents and problems. You'll perform root cause analysis, implement corrective actions, and proactively identify areas for system optimisation to enhance performance and user experience.

Project Leadership & Collaboration: Play a vital role in SAP-related projects, including upgrades, migrations, and new module implementations. You'll work seamlessly with other IT teams (e.g., Basis, ABAP developers), external consultants, and business users to deliver successful outcomes.




We're looking for someone who brings:

You're a highly experienced and technically proficient SAP professional with a deep understanding of supply chain processes and a passion for optimizing systems. You're a natural problem-solver, an excellent communicator, and thrive in a dynamic, multicultural environment.

We're looking for someone who brings:

Higher education in Computer Sciences / Any Engineering Studies, and related SAP certification is mandatory.

Minimum of 10 years of hands-on/proven experience in SAP SCM functional roles covering Materials Management (MM), Production Planning (PP), Sales and Distribution (SD), and Warehouse Management (WM).

Proven experience in full life-cycle SAP implementations, including gathering requirements, configuring systems, conducting testing, and supporting go-live.

Experience in S/4HANA System conversion is a must.

Proficient and strong understanding of supply chain processes such as procurement, inventory management, production planning, and order fulfilment.

Knowledge of SQL and PL/SQL, with experience in Manhattan Scale highly preferred.


Ability to write functional specifications for custom developments and support ABAP teams as needed.

Experience with SAP interfaces, integrations, and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) with Manhattan Scale is beneficial.




Why Join Us?

Collaborative work environment fostering a positive team culture.

Competitive salary + Superannuation + Performance bonus.

Equipment for work will be provided, such as a work laptop and work phone.

Provided with on-the-job training for continuous career growth opportunities.

Job Title: Lecturer/Senior Lecturer, Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience
Ad Text:
About the Role 
Swinburne is seeking a Lecturer or Senior Lecturer in Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience to join our high-performing academic team. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to world-class teaching, impactful research, and innovative curriculum design within a collaborative and future-focused environment.

Depending on your experience, you will contribute at either Lecturer or Senior Lecturer level across teaching, research, and curriculum development in cognitive neuroscience. In this role, you will:

Deliver engaging undergraduate and postgraduate teaching
Design and enhance contemporary, industry-relevant curriculum
Conduct high-quality, impactful research
Supervise undergraduate, postgraduate, and higher degree research students
Contribute to academic administration and professional activities

At Senior Lecturer level, you will also:

Lead courses and coordinate teaching teams
Provide academic leadership and mentorship to colleagues and students
Foster stronger links with industry and external partners (national and international)
Create and expand opportunities for student engagement in research beyond coursework

About the Department
The Department of Psychological Sciences, within the School of Health Sciences, is recognised for excellence in both teaching and research. Our research is nationally and internationally recognised and we continue to advance innovation in psychology and cognitive neuroscience.

We work closely with the Centre for Mental Health and Brain Sciences and Swinburne’s world-leading Neuroimaging Facility—the only single-site human imaging facility in Australia and New Zealand offering MEG, MRI, and EEG.


About You  
You are a collaborative and motivated academic with expertise in cognitive neuroscience and a passion for teaching and research. For both levels of appointment, you will bring:

Proven research experience using cognitive neuroscience methodologies (e.g. MRI, MEG, EEG)
A strong (Lecturer) or established (Senior Lecturer) track record of publications
Experience teaching in a university setting
Ability to contribute across a broad range of undergraduate and postgraduate offerings
Excellent communication skills and a collaborative approach

In addition, at Senior Lecturer level you will demonstrate:

Leadership experience in higher education, including course or program coordination

Ad Text:
Permanent Opportunity
Hybrid Work Environment
Lead a delivery practice supporting enterprise integration solutions for national critical systems


About the Role


As a Practice Manager supporting the Integration Services team at the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA), you will lead, coach and develop team members while working closely with project managers, technical professionals, architecture and security teams to achieve agreed delivery outcomes.


You will be responsible for establishing and maintaining standard work practices and integration design standards, including automation, to improve the quality, maturity and consistency of services, technologies and supporting processes.


Key Responsibilities


Develop, lead and mature the Integration Services delivery practice by providing people leadership, building capability, setting direction and priorities, managing demand forecasts with supply and contributing to resourcing strategies that enable high quality delivery outcomes.
Ensure operational accountability for the delivery practice by overseeing performance, compliance and adherence to agreed processes and controls.
Provide technical delivery guidance and assurance across integration initiatives, ensuring solutions meet agreed standards for quality, consistency, reliability, and operational readiness.
Partner closely with project managers, architecture, security, operations, and vendors to guide solution design, align expectations, support accurate estimation and forecasting, resolve delivery and technical risks, and achieve sustainable outcomes.
Influence and promote the adoption of common integration patterns, platforms, and automation approaches to improve reuse, efficiency, and long term maintainability.
Contribute to the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of integration standards, policies, systems, and delivery practices, ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture, industry standards, and organisational requirements.
Ensure integration solutions are delivered and transitioned to meet operational needs, supportability, sustainability, enabling effective transition into business as usual operations.
Manage stakeholder and vendor relationships, ensuring procurement and delivery engagements are effective, expectations are aligned, and issues or blockers impacting delivery outcomes are addressed in a timely manner.
Proactively identify, assess, and respond to delivery, technical, and operational risks and opportunities, maintaining delivery momentum while protecting service quality and stability.
Provide clear assurance, insight, and reporting on integration delivery outcomes, risks, and improvement opportunities to senior stakeholders.


About You


You bring strong people and practice leadership experience, including developing capability, setting clear expectations, and fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and accountability.
You are an experienced technology and delivery leader with a proven ability to influence technical outcomes across complex initiatives, balancing delivery priorities with long term sustainability, operability, and risk management.
You have a solid understanding of integration technologies and patterns across hybrid environments, including APIs, data and application integration, and middleware platforms, and are comfortable guiding and challenging solution design without being hands‑on.
You bring experience implementing, evolving, or scaling enterprise integration platforms in complex environments, including technologies such as IBM Sterling Integrator, MQ, MuleSoft, Azure Logic Apps, and Azure API Management.
You are experienced in governing and evolving delivery practices, standards, patterns, and automation approaches to improve quality, consistency, and efficiency across multiple initiatives.
You work effectively with technical professionals within your practice, as well as project managers, architecture, operations, and vendors, influencing decisions and resolving delivery and technical risks.
You bring a strong understanding of Financial Services, Government, or similarly regulated environments, including relevant policies, risk frameworks, and compliance obligations, and can guide teams to deliver compliant outcomes.
You understand the importance of operational readiness, and can ensure solutions are supportable, reliable, and fit for ongoing operation.
You bring a pragmatic understanding of portfolio and project delivery, applying waterfall and agile approaches to enable effective delivery rather than enforcing methodology.
You have strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with experience providing assurance and clear advice to senior leaders.


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.
