Ad Text:
The Opportunity

Join a high‑performing Insurance Capability Team that plays a critical role in strengthening the performance, sustainability and integrity of the ReturntoWork Scheme in South Australia.

As the Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor, you will lead strategic, scheme‑wide capability initiatives that enable Insurance leaders and teams - including our Claims Agents - to deliver improved outcomes for injured workers and employers. This role is central to identifying capability risks, designing practical and strategic solutions, and embedding systems that build a skilled, sustainable workforce across the Scheme.

Working closely with ReturntoWorkSA leaders, Claims Agents, internal business units and external stakeholders, you will turn strategic intent into operational reality. You will design and operationalise capability frameworks, lead complex projects, and provide strategic insights that support informed decision‑making across the Insurance function.

If you are energised by strategic problem‑solving, enjoy influencing at senior levels and want to make a meaningful impact on the performance of the Scheme, we encourage you to apply.

This role is offered as a two-year, full-time contract position.

The Position

As an Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor, you will:

Optimise Insurance Capability

Design and maintain strategic systems that identify and analyse capability gaps across Insurance and the broader Scheme
Lead strategic initiatives that address capability risks and strengthen operational performance
Contribute to the design and review of key claims management systems and tools
Develop practical resources to support the application of legislative tests
Research best‑practice capability approaches across comparable schemes
Partner with People & Culture to identify talent needs and workforce risks

Enable Insurance Leadership Strategy

Work with Insurance leaders to evaluate scheme performance and identify emerging risks
Provide insights, tools, forums and data that support timely and informed strategic decisions
Support leaders to build strategic capability and deliver on Insurance strategic

Capability Data Management & Analysis

Establish and maintain robust capability and customer service data systems including Insurance Customer Experience
Produce monthly reporting and work with leaders to analyse trends and emerging risks
Develop and enable strategies to address identified risks

About You

You are a proactive and experienced strategic leader who is motivated by improving performance through people, systems and capability. You bring a balance of analytical thinking, operational understanding and strong stakeholder influence.

You will bring:

Expert knowledge of insurance scheme(s) and/or workers compensation (SA scheme experience desirable)
Experience designing and leading strategic programs or projects at a senior level
Strong research, analytical and problem‑solving capability
High‑level communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to translate complex insights into practical, actionable solutions
Experience navigating organisational, legislative or technological change
Strong planning, organisation and governance capability
Tertiary qualifications in business, commerce, HR, law or a related field (preferred)

Further information

Candidates will also be assessed based on their ability to demonstrate personal and professional alignment with ReturnToWorkSA’s organisational values which are critical to our success: We care about people and sustainability; We are inclusive and innovative; We listen to understand; and We own our actions.

Remuneration

The total remuneration package for this Senior Officer position starts from $162,700.00 (inclusive of superannuation).

The final offer will be commensurate with the successful candidate’s qualifications, skills and experience.

 Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor - Job Description

How to Apply

Please submit a cover letter demonstrating your key capabilities and experience, supported by your current resume, addressed to Jayne Gowland by 5:00pm, 29 April 2026.

For further information, please contact recruitment@rtwsa.com or phone Jayne on 0438 376 011.

Shortlisting and interviews will commence immediately, and the role may close early if a preferred candidate is identified.

About ReturnToWorkSA

At ReturnToWorkSA, our purpose is to empower and support South Australians impacted by work injury. Our services and those delivered by our claims agents are designed to provide early intervention support to workers and employers following a work injury to ensure the worker can recover and return to work as quickly as possible. We manage the collection of premium payments from registered employers and our invested funds to ensure we maintain a viable financial position that enables the delivery of quality support and services to injured workers.

In addition, we are the regulator of the Return to Work Scheme and play the key role of protecting the integrity and fairness of the Scheme by monitoring and enforcing compliance with the Act and providing education and support about the health benefits of work.

Why join us? 

Experience a workplace culture that rewards your contribution and provides the development and support to help you reach your potential. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can genuinely make a difference to the lives of South Australians affected by work injury. At ReturnToWorkSA, you can expect a range of benefits including:

An extensive wellbeing program
access to a broad range of wellbeing seminars, webinars and information sessions
a free onsite gym and bike storage
annual vaccinations, skin checks, seasonal fruit and corporate cup challenge
discounted corporate private health insurance
and a proactive EAP provider
Flexible work options including part time and working from home
Access to learning and development programs to enhance your personal and professional skill set and career development
Modern offices with excellent amenities in the Adelaide CBD

Inclusion and acceptance

ReturnToWorkSA is an equal opportunity employer, valuing difference and embracing diversity and we promote flexible ways of working, applicants are encouraged to discuss any workplace adjustments that might be needed as well as the flexible working arrangements for this role.

Still not sure?

We are proud to be recognised as an Endorsed Employer for All Women by WORK180. Research indicates that up to 60% of women and underrepresented groups may hesitate to proceed, even after completing a draft application. We firmly believe that diversity enhances the strength of every team, so even if you don’t tick all the boxes we still want to hear from you.

Are you ready to shape the future of data architecture and help build a modern, scalable, and insights-driven enterprise?

We’re seeking an experienced Senior Data Architect to lead the design and delivery of next-generation data solutions — from data lakes and integration frameworks to advanced analytics platforms.

As the Senior Data Architect, you’ll play a key role in defining and delivering the organisation’s enterprise data architecture. In addition, you’ll ensure data is structured, governed, and leveraged effectively to support operational and strategic decision-making.

You’ll provide architectural leadership across integration, data warehousing, analytics, and governance while fostering collaboration between business units, technical teams, and senior leadership.

Key Responsibilities of the Senior Data Architect will include:
Develop and maintain enterprise-wide data architecture strategies, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with business goals.
Lead solution design for data platforms, integrations, data lakes, and BI/reporting systems using modern architectures (batch, streaming, APIs, microservices).
Create conceptual, logical, and physical data models across structured and unstructured data using best-practice frameworks (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault).
Define and enforce data standards for quality, metadata, security, privacy, and compliance.
Architect data solutions across Azure, Snowflake, Informatica, and hybrid/multi-cloud environments.
Collaborate with stakeholders and technical teams to translate business needs into data-driven solutions.
Guide and mentor engineering teams on CI/CD, automation, and modern data engineering practices.
Optimise data systems for performance, scalability, and reliability.
Promote and embed a strong data culture across the organisation.
Skills & Experience required to measure success:
7+ years’ experience in data architecture, solution design, and enterprise data management.
Proven success architecting large-scale BI, analytics, and integration platforms.
Strong knowledge of cloud and hybrid ecosystems (Azure, AWS, Snowflake, Informatica, Databricks).
Expertise in data modelling (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault 2.0).
Experience with data engineering tools, CI/CD, and DevOps practices.
Deep understanding of governance, metadata, and master data management.
Experience with modern integration approaches (APIs, event-driven, streaming).
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and leadership capability

Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field (required).
Postgraduate qualification in Data Science or Information Systems (highly regarded).
Certifications in Microsoft, Azure, Snowflake, or Informatica (desirable).
Apply now to shameem.prinsloo@troocoo.com if you’re passionate about leading enterprise-scale data architecture and building data ecosystems that enable real business impact!

Company description:



The Metcash story began from the humble origins of our first family-owned corner store in 1927. And for almost 100 years since then, we've been backing independents and strengthening the communities we operate in.

Today, Metcash (ASX: MTS) is Australia and New Zealand's leading independent retail partner and wholesaler in the food, liquor, hardware, and tools industries, with sales of $19.5 billion in FY25. We provide our network with marketing, purchasing, warehousing, distribution and systems support



Job description:



At Metcash, you're part of something bigger. We've got the heart of a small business with the strength and support of a big business.

When you join Metcash, your work makes a real difference to independent businesses and local communities, empowering you to:

Grow your career with tailored development programs
Thrive your way with flexible work options
Make your mark in a high-performing, values-led team

Australian Liquor Marketers (ALM) is the Liquor wholesale division of Metcash. We are the largest wide range wholesaler to the independent liquor trade. Being part of something bigger in the ALM team is all about making a huge difference for the local retailers, businesses and communities we service. And while you're championing successful independents, the wider Metcash family is behind you with a sea of opportunities to develop yourself, grow your career, and make your mark.

About the Opportunity

Reporting into the Senior FP & A Manager, the Senior FP&A Analyst delivers high impact financial insights and forward-looking analysis. The successful individual will drive commercial performance across the ALM pillar, while strengthening financial processes and reporting to support faster, better decision making. This is a full-time, permanent role based at the Macquarie Park office, with three days per week onsite.

In this Role

Identify EBIT/Gross Revenue ratio improvement opportunities, including cost savings and revenue mix optimisation.
In conjunction with Senior FP&A Manager partner and cross functional partners assist in modelling and developing longer term strategic objectives
Review YTD performance and provide YTG recommendations at state and customer level based on risks and opportunities.
Partner with State teams to update forecasts with future costs in line with latest performance.
Analyse and provide insights on sales variances to budget/forecast, highlighting customer under/overperformance and risks.
Analyse rebate variances, including product and supplier mix impacts.
Provide insights and recommendations for cost of sales over/underspend and identify ongoing risks or opportunities.
Consolidate state balance sheets, review variances, and provide visibility of key movements.
Prepare QBR decks and monthly performance insights.

You're Likely a Match If

Proven FP&A experience within an FMCG Company - Liquor Industry advantageous
Demonstrated experience of leading budgeting and forecasting including the ability to challenge and influence outcomes
Strong level of business partnering with Senior Management
Strong understanding of financial processes & P&L (Dr/Cr, BS, cashflow)
Power BI experience desirable

What's on offer

📚 Career Growth: Endless learning and development opportunities

About Us

Join us and be part of something bigger!

Metcash is Australia's leading wholesale distribution company, with $19.5 billion in sales (FY25) and a proud spot in the ASX Top 125.

We believe in the power of independent, family-owned businesses to shape vibrant communities. Through our food, liquor, and hardware pillars, we help retailers become the ‘Best Store in Their Town' by providing merchandising, operational and marketing support.

We're committed to diversity, inclusion, and sustainability — and we welcome applicants from all backgrounds, including First Nations. If you need support during the application process, just let us know. Your privacy and dignity are always respected.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

The Compliance Program Reporting Lead plays a pivotal role in supporting Optus' Compliance Program by delivering high‑quality, executive‑ready reporting and insights across a complex regulatory environment. Reporting to the Director, Delivery Lead, this role acts as the central point for compliance program reporting, providing clear visibility of program progress, compliance status, risks and issues.

Responsible for owning and standardizing reporting frameworks, templates and artefacts, the role partners closely with project and business teams to embed consistent, transparent reporting practices. Working alongside Compliance, Legal and delivery teams, the position also supports the practical application of the Compliance Management Framework, monitors adherence to regulatory obligations and facilitates timely escalation and resolution of issues, balancing regulatory rigour with pragmatic, business‑aligned outcomes.

Your day to day will consist of

Own and maintain Compliance Program reporting standards, templates and artefacts, ensuring consistent, accurate and executive‑ready outputs

Collate, validate and analyse compliance and program data to produce regular and ad‑hoc reports for senior leadership, governance forums and regulators

Partner closely with project managers and business stakeholders to support timely, high‑quality reporting and embed strong reporting disciplines

Monitor project alignment with the Compliance Management Framework, identifying risks, issues and early warning indicators, and escalating as required

Drive continuous improvement in reporting processes, data quality and insights, working with Compliance, Legal and PMO teams to support effective program delivery

What makes you perfect for the role?

Extensive experience in compliance program reporting, delivering clear, executive‑ready insights across complex, highly regulated environments

Strong expertise in establishing and improving reporting frameworks, standards and governance artefacts to support senior leadership and regulatory requirements

Proven ability to partner with project managers, Compliance, Legal and PMO teams to embed consistent reporting disciplines and compliance practices

Highly analytical, with a track record of identifying risks, trends and early warning indicators through data‑driven insights

Confident stakeholder influencer, able to balance regulatory rigor with pragmatic, commercially aligned advice across all levels of the organization

The good stuff….

About Us

Future Secure AI is a rapidly scaling AI company co‑developed with one of the world’s largest financial institutions. Operating with the agility of a startup and the backing of global enterprise leaders, we are building technology that elevates human performance through advanced AI Co-workers and next‑generation LLM systems.

With deep C‑suite access across industries and a growing network of global partners, our mission is bold: to redefine how humans and AI collaborate—and deliver 10x outcomes for individuals and organisations.

We are growing fast and looking for exceptional talent to help shape the future of AI.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be a key technical leader within our advanced analytics and AI group. You will design, build, and deploy sophisticated machine learning systems—particularly LLM‑driven solutions—that solve high‑value, real‑world problems for enterprise clients.

You’ll be part of a culture that prizes technical excellence, creativity, and continuous learning. Beyond technical depth, you will influence strategy, mentor emerging talent, and help shape our approach to responsible and scalable AI.

Your work will directly contribute to AI systems that have the potential to transform industries—and society.

What You’ll Do

Architect, build, and deploy machine learning models and LLM systems that drive meaningful impact for our products and users

Analyse complex, large‑scale structured and unstructured datasets to uncover insights and opportunities

Enhance LLM output quality across diverse use cases, including optimisation of multi‑step reasoning chains

Lead MLOps initiatives including model performance tracking, benchmark design, quality assurance, and compliance

Influence long‑term data and AI strategy, including governance, ethical AI, and scalable frameworks

Collaborate closely with engineering, product, and executive stakeholders to deliver innovative, production‑ready solutions

Mentor and uplift junior data scientists, setting standards for excellence

About You

You are a seasoned Data Scientist (5+ years) with deep hands‑on experience and a track record of delivering impactful ML and AI outcomes. You thrive in ambiguity, communicate exceptionally well, and enjoy partnering with cross‑functional teams to deliver measurable results.

You balance scientific rigour with practical execution, and you bring a curiosity that keeps you constantly exploring new research—especially in the fast‑moving LLM space.

You’ll be successful if you have:

A Master’s or Doctorate in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science or a related discipline

Experience from within AI sector, ideally agentic AI

Strong experience with NLP techniques: LSTMs, CNNs, transformers, topic modelling, supervised NLP

Commercial experience with modern LLM ecosystems (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG pipelines, multi‑agent frameworks, GPT‑based systems)

Python experience focused on machine learning and NLP. Knowledge of other software languages is highly beneficial

Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical stakeholders

Empathy, humility, and a growth mindset—open to feedback and committed to collaboration

Comfort working in an agile, fast‑evolving environment

Ability to attend our Sydney CBD office 1–2 days per week

Why Join Us?

Shape the future of AI alongside world‑class technical and commercial leaders

Work with cutting‑edge models, frameworks, and R&D pipelines

Contribute to solutions used by global enterprises

Grow rapidly in a company with significant backing and momentum

Join a high‑trust, high‑performance culture built around craft, ownership, and impact

If you’re driven by innovation, inspired by big challenges, and ready to build AI systems that matter—we’d love to meet you.

For a quicker response, we encourage you to apply directly through our website at https://www.futuresecure.ai/. There, you can watch a video about AI workers and explore the career opportunities we have available.

Job Title: Senior Vice President of Retail Products
Ad Text:

Best Places to Work — Business Intelligence Group (3 consecutive years)

2025 Great Place To Work® Australia Certified™

Competitive bonus structure

Flexible hybrid working arrangements

Daily lunch in the office

Monthly wellbeing program subscription

Strong commercial orientation, with experience defining and influencing pricing, positioning, and revenue outcomes. 

Experience leading and developing high-performing teams. 

Technical fluency in trading infrastructure (connectivity, execution workflows, risk management, APIs, data pipelines) is a strong plus. 

Comfort and skill in speaking at industry events or interviews 

Bachelor’s Degree in technical, hard science, or business fields.  Advanced degrees are a plus.

True rewards

The role
You will provide expert advice and guidance to clients, helping them achieve their business, IT and customer goals.

Your role will involve working closely with clients to identify their needs and developing strategies to address those needs.

This may include analysing data, creating new processes and procedures, aligning business requirements with technical delivery, and identifying opportunities for improvement.

Independently lead and deliver client engagements, effectively managing timelines, budgets and forecasted effort.
Collaborate closely with clients and internal teams to understand business challenges and design practical tailored solutions.

Design digital, IT and AI strategies aligned to organisational priorities, risk profiles and long-term business objectives.

What We’re Looking For

Strong experience in successfully leading consulting engagements that have produced positive and tangible business outcomes.
Ability to develop, lead and facilitate stakeholder workshops to identify problems and develop solutions using contemporary approaches.
A strong client focus and a strength in taking ownership to proactively monitor customer satisfaction while negotiating mutually beneficial outcomes.

Extensive experience of the broader technology ecosystem, common approaches to managing technology, modern technology concepts and emerging technologies.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

About the team

Our Finance team are a vital partner in every aspect of our business, providing commercial insights to drive our success and influence everything we do. With presence in every department, we analyse performance, and deliver actionable insights that shape our strategic decisions.

About the role


As the Finance Business Partner - Sustainability, you will be providing trusted financial partnership to support Coles’ ESG and sustainability targets, embedding sustainability within our commercial teams. Your key responsibilities will include:

Partner with Commercial Sustainability as a trusted Finance Business Partner, providing financial information, analysis, tools and insights to support informed decision-making and business strategy.

Ensure ESG objectives are embedded across planning, capital allocation, risk management and decision-making, supporting delivery of Coles’ externally committed sustainability targets.

Deliver monthly and quarterly financial reporting for senior forums including LT sessions, Commercial Leadership Team packs and Quarterly Business Reviews.
Build strong stakeholder alignment across Finance and Commercial teams, engaging with GMs, Heads of, Senior Finance Managers, FBPs, project managers and cross-functional partners across risk, strategy and financial control.

About you and your skills



You are a commercially savvy finance professional, with a strong outcome mindset, capable of simplifying complex sustainability and financial information for senior stakeholders across large, complex organisations. The ideal candidate will have:



CA, CPA or equivalent professional accounting qualification.
Experience in a business partnering role, ideally within retail, FMCG or environments with enterprise‑wide sustainability commitments.
Familiarity with climate change economics and decarbonisation levers desirable.
Demonstrated experience with sustainability decisions impacting margin and pricing.
Strong exposure to ESG frameworks, climate risk and investment decisions.
Proven ability to analyse data and translate it into meaningful, actionable commercial insights.
Experience producing and presenting insightful commercial and financial reports, including recommendations to support decision-making.
Background in implementing change and continuous improvement initiatives that deliver value and improved business outcomes.

Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

Job Title: Data Modeller - Payroll Compliance and Remediation
Ad Text:
PM-Partners group is a specialist Project Management Consultancy. We have a unique way of running projects and providing training and advice, so our clients get the best results with less cost and risk. Each year we deliver over $3B of projects, train over 10,000 people, and help some of Australia’s leading brands improve their PMOs.




Engagement: 6 month fixed term contract 

Employment Type: Full Time 38 hours

Work Location: Remote or Sydney

Start Date: ASAP




About the Role

As a Data Modeller, you will support payroll remediation initiatives by designing and delivering high-quality data models that enable accurate underpayment analysis and back-pay calculations.

You will develop payroll remediation models using Snowflake SQL, aligned to industrial relations obligations and legislative requirements, to identify compliance gaps and calculate potential underpayments. Leveraging your expertise in Australian payroll, you will translate Awards, Enterprise Agreements, and IR pay rules into structured, auditable data assets that support transparent calculations, reconciliation, and regulatory compliance.




Ideal Candidates

Ideally looking for someone who has work in a software delivery space with:

3-5+ years’ experience in data modelling, data engineering, or analytics roles, with a strong focus on complex, time-based data
Demonstrated experience on Australian payroll remediation, payroll transformation, or underpayment programs, with hands-on involvement in historical recalculations.
Strong Australian payroll domain knowledge, including:
Modern awards and/or Enterprise Agreements
Penalties, overtime, allowances, loadings
Averaging arrangements and minimum engagement rules
Superannuation (OTE vs non-OTE) and basic tax/charges concepts E.g Payroll tax, SGC, PAYG
Proven ability to design logical and physical data models that support award and pay rule calculations.
Advanced SQL skills, with experience preparing calculation-ready datasets for rule engines or large-scale recalculation logic.
Experience integrating and reconciling data from payroll systems and time-and-attendance platforms.
Strong understanding of effective-dated data, historical rate changes, and lookback period recalculations
Experience producing audit-ready documentation, including data dictionaries, mappings, Decisions and assumptions.

Core Responsibilities

Design and maintain calculation-ready payroll data models to support large-scale payroll remediation activities.
Structure and transform payroll, time and attendance, and HR data into a canonical remediation model suitable for historical recalculations.
Prepare data assets that support:
Award and Enterprise Agreement pay rules
Penalty, overtime, and allowance calculations
Apportionment of hours and pay across time boundaries
Averaging arrangements and higher-of comparisons
Recalculation across multi-year remediation lookback periods
Analyse and map data from multiple payroll and timekeeping systems, documenting source-to-target mappings and transformation logic.
Ensure payroll data is effective-dated, auditable, and traceable for quality assurance requirements.
Implement data quality checks and support reconciliation of recalculated outcomes against original payroll payments or compliance models.
Produce clear documentation including data dictionaries, model diagrams, and assumptions to support audit, legal, and Fair Work scrutiny.
Work collaboratively with IR, Legal, Payroll, and remediation calculation teams to validate data outputs and resolve data issues.
Deliver agreed modelling and data outputs within scope, timelines, and governance frameworks typical of payroll remediation programs.

If you feel you meet our selection criteria, please submit your resume via SEEK application

ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking an experienced and commercially astute Pricing Leader to join Ampcontrol. This role can be based out of North Sydney or Tomago.

This is a pivotal, enterprise‑wide role responsible for transforming how Ampcontrol prices its Engineer‑to‑Order (ETO) projects, products and services. You will lead the shift from traditional cost‑plus pricing to a more strategic, market‑ and value‑based pricing approach, embedding pricing as a core commercial capability across the organisation.

Partnering closely with Sales, Engineering, Finance and Operations, you will provide strategic pricing leadership, support complex bids and tenders, improve margin performance, and strengthen Ampcontrol’s competitiveness as we continue to grow organically and through M&A.

If you are passionate about value creation, commercial discipline and driving meaningful change in complex environments, we’d love to hear from you.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Lead the transformation from cost‑plus to market‑ and value‑based pricing across ETO projects, products and services.
Develop and implement pricing strategies, frameworks and governance that improve margin performance and commercial discipline.
Provide strategic pricing leadership and commercial support for complex bids, tenders and high‑value opportunities.
Design scalable pricing models for ETO, standard, configurable and services offerings, incorporating risk and value insights.
Analyse customer, market and competitor pricing trends to inform go‑to‑market and deal decisions.
Build pricing capability across the business by coaching, enabling and partnering with Sales, Engineering, Finance and Operations.

ABOUT YOU

Education & Qualifications

Technical Skills

Job Title: EL2 - Director, Data Strategy and Partnerships
Company: Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport and the Arts
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
The opportunity we have available

We are seeking an experienced, enthusiastic and motivated Director who can successfully drive the strategic engagement and delivery activities for the Data Strategy and Partnerships section (DSP). DSP leads delivery of the department’s Data Strategy 2025-29 in partnership with business areas and supports the Chief Data Officer to represent the department in cross government data forums. The section works to embed a strong data use culture, uplift enterprise data capability, and improve how data is managed as a strategic business asset and artificial intelligence readiness. We seek candidates who have experience in, and an understanding of, the critical role data plays. Demonstrated experience in leadership and data activities to achieve excellent outcomes will be an advantage.

What will you do?

Key activities as part of the role include leading a team to:

Implement the Data Strategy 2025-29 in partnership with key departmental stakeholders
Deliver projects to implement transformational data initiatives across the department
Develop rolling annual Data Strategy Action Plans to uplift departmental data capability
Support the CDO and Indigenous Data Champion to implement departmental data governance and management and the Framework for Governance of Indigenous Data
Champion data capability uplift initiatives
Provide briefings and advice on a broad range of data matters to departmental and external stakeholders, including senior executives
Provide formal advice to the Executive and Government, such as Cabinet submissions and proposals for new policies and programs.

Who are we looking for?

The successful candidate for this position will be able to demonstrate their capability against the EL2 Work level standards. This includes demonstrating a positive attitude, resourcefulness, initiative, collaboration and flexibility to deliver results. Successful candidates will have strong leadership skills, excellent communication skills, sound judgement, strong stakeholder engagement skills, strong organisation and project management skills, and the ability to manage complexity and deliver quality outcomes.

Experience leading teams responsible for enterprise capability uplift initiatives, or strong project management experience, is highly regarded. Tertiary qualifications in data governance, data management, data science, data analytics, information systems, or a related discipline are desirable, but not essential. We strongly encourage applications from candidates who have built relevant capability through professional experience, have led departmental initiatives, or have a strong interest in the value and effective use of data.

To be successful in this role you will need to demonstrate your ability to:

Lead a team to achieve outcomes in a complex environment.
Think strategically, evaluating complex information from a variety of sources to make connections and sound decisions while exercising good judgement.
Support the team and the CDO to achieve results and quality outcomes by showing initiative and leadership, working in a small group and delivering multiple activities at any given time, often within tight timeframes.
Communicate effectively, both verbally and in writing, delivering information in a variety of formats and for a range of audiences.
Collaborate across diverse teams using your advanced interpersonal and engagement skills to engage with senior leaders and a wide variety of stakeholders, valuing different perspectives, contributions and backgrounds.
Have a growth mindset with a willingness to continue to learn, develop and foster an environment for team members to do the same.

We encourage candidates from diverse backgrounds and experiences to apply.

Cultural capability requirement

The department believes every role contributes to cultural safety and that building staff cultural capability helps us deliver better policies, programs and services for First Nations people and the broader Australian community. Candidates will be required to demonstrate their willingness to develop their cultural capability as part of the assessment process. In making it a requirement across all roles in our department, we are taking a practical step in delivering our commitments under the Our Stories on Country Agreement and the Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Strategy 2025–28.

As an EL2 you will be expected to:

Provide strategic leadership to embed cultural capability across programs, policies or functions.
Influence senior leaders on culturally informed approaches.
Drive partnerships and co-design with Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander organisations and communities where applicable or appropriate.

Eligibility requirements

Employment with the Department of Infrastructure, Transport, Regional Development, Communications, Sport and the Arts is subject to conditions prescribed within the Public Service Act 1999 including:

Citizenship: candidates must be an Australian citizen at the time of submitting your application.
Health Assessment: The preferred candidate may be required to undergo a medical examination conducted by the department’s preferred medical provider.
Security Clearance: The successful candidate must be able to obtain and/or maintain a security clearance at Baseline. You must be willing to disclose all relevant and required information. You must have lived in Australia, or have a checkable background, for at least the preceding five years for Baseline Vetting clearances. More information on the security clearance vetting process is available on the Australian Government Security Vetting Agency (AGSVA) website.
Police check: The successful candidate must satisfactory complete an Australian Federal Police criminal history check.
Integrity and Performance check: The successful candidate must satisfy the requirements in an Integrity and Performance check which is completed by your most recent employer.
Qualifications: Tertiary qualifications in data science, analytics, information systems, information technology, computer science, statistics, public policy, or a related discipline, or equivalent senior‑level experience is desirable but not essential.

Salary: Be the finance partner behind our supply chain
Ad Text:
About the Role:

Exciting opportunity for a commercially minded Finance Business Partner to join our Supply Chain Finance team, supporting our Logistics and Distribution operations across the network. This role sits at the heart of the business, partnering closely with operational leaders to drive performance, optimise costs, and support strategic decision-making.

You will play a key role in driving business performance and cost optimisation by delivering meaningful financial insights and supporting decision-making. The role requires translating complex financial data into clear, actionable insights for operational stakeholders. With a strong focus on forecasting, reporting, and variance analysis, you will partner closely with Logistics teams to optimise cost performance while balancing operational realities and strategic objectives.

This is a highly collaborative role suited to a commercial finance professional who enjoys influencing beyond finance and driving tangible operational outcomes.

What your days look like:

• Partnering with Logistics, Warehousing and Distribution teams to provide financial insight and decision support
• Leading budgeting (AOP), forecasting and monthly reporting for logistics and distribution costs
• Delivering variance analysis across freight, transport, warehousing, labour and productivity
• Developing financial models for scenario planning and cost optimisation initiatives
• Monitoring and reporting key KPIs such as cost per case, cost per delivery and warehouse productivity
• Translating financial data into clear, actionable insights for operational leaders
• Driving standardisation, automation and continuous improvement in reporting processes
• Supporting ad hoc analysis and cross‑functional projects as required

The Ingredients for Success:

Just like strong decisions rely on great data, there are a few essential ingredients we need for this role:

• 5-7 years' experience in finance, ideally within FMCG, manufacturing or supply chain environments
• Proven financial business partnering experience with operational stakeholders
• Strong ability to influence, challenge and support non‑finance teams
• Advanced financial modelling and Excel capability
• Highly analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into insight
• Strong understanding of logistics and distribution cost drivers (preferred)
• Experience with ERP systems and reporting tools (e.g. SAP, Power BI)
• Clear and confident communication skills, both written and verbal

Perks:

• Discounted Gym Memberships
• 12 Weeks Paid Parental Leave for all new parents
• Free onsite parking
• Discounted Products
• Discounted Health Insurance
• NAWO membership

Our Culture:

Our purpose is simple - to create delicious moments. We stand for high performance with integrity and value strong partnerships, clear accountability and continuous improvement. You'll be empowered to challenge, influence and make an impact while working alongside passionate people who are committed to delivering great outcomes.

Are You Ready to Make It Happen at Mondelēz International? 

Join our Mission to Lead the Future of Snacking. Make It Uniquely Yours. 

Mondelēz International empowers people to snack right in over 150 countries around the world. We're leading the future of snacking with iconic global and local brands such as Oreo, Cadbury, Philadelphia, Pascall, The Natural Confectionery Company, Belvita, Ritz and Toblerone with global revenues exceeding $36 billion USD.    

 

About the Role 

Join our team and play a pivotal role in driving profitable growth by leading the development and execution of robust customer plans. You will be instrumental in translating strategic initiatives into tactical approaches, ensuring seamless implementation across our sales, category, marketing, and finance teams. This collaborative effort is key to achieving our ambitious annual targets. 

This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated Finance Analyst or Manager eager to gain hands-on commercial experience, or for an experienced Account Manager looking to significantly deepen their expertise in P&L management and financial acumen within a fast-paced environment. 

 

How you will contribute 

Develop the pricing strategy for our Gifting & Seasonal portfolio, utilising all RGM levers and partnering with sales, marketing, and category planning to implement the strategy.

Understands market dynamics / consumer insights by channel to participate in trade marketing strategic decisions.

Understand and synthesise insights on category pricing activities across competitor price points, formats, and pack groups to unlock paths to category growth.

Lead alignment meetings with the Sales team on pricing plans.

Partner with category revenue teams to shape the financial delivery of your portfolios.

 

What you will bring 

Demonstrated experience in strategic category planning and driving growth through effective category leadership. 

A future-focused and curious mindset, with the ability to identify industry trends, leverage system solutions, and translate these insights into actionable business plans. 

Proficiency in developing and executing comprehensive business plans, including the measurement and monitoring of results, and formulating recommendations to achieve ambitious growth targets. 

Strong understanding of business planning principles and proven ability to maximise revenue growth. 

Exceptional stakeholder influencing skills, coupled with the ability to interact effectively across various functions and the resilience to present alternative viewpoints when necessary. 

Highly developed analytical skills and strong business acumen. 




No Relocation support available.

Business Unit Summary

At Mondelez International, our mission is to provide consumers with the right snack, for the right moment, made in the right way. We are one of the largest snack companies in the world, with global net revenues exceeding $31 billion USD. 

We have operations in more than 80 countries and employ approximately 91,000 diverse and talented employees in our factories, offices, research & development facilities and distribution activities around the world.

In Australia and New Zealand, we have 6 factories, and both global and local brands, employing over 2,000 people; Cadbury Dairy Milk, Oreo, Toblerone, Philadelphia, belVita, Pascall, The Natural Confectionery Company, Sour Patch Kids and Ritz.

Mondelēz International is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation or preference, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Who is Plenti? 

Plenti is a fintech lender, providing faster, fairer loans by leveraging its smart technology. Plenti is a dynamic and innovative business that is growing strongly. By continuing to deliver better customer experiences, Plenti is taking market share from incumbent players in the personal lending, renewable energy, and automotive finance markets.

We are a fast moving and ambitious business that seeks to recruit smart and capable people, who can take ownership of their role to help the business thrive. With over 250 people based in Australia, Plenti is of a size where everyone can make a difference in their role and help us realise our very big ambitions as a team, as we go about building Australia’s best lender.

Plenti is a founder led business that launched in 2014, listed on the ASX since 2020 with annual revenue of over $250 million and a loan portfolio of over $2.5 billion.


About the Role:
As a Senior Product Manager, you will co-lead our Finance & Servicing platform domain, driving the roadmap and delivery of the systems that underpin financial operations, regulatory reporting and servicing. This role offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of product, engineering and finance, solving complex platform challenges at scale. 

Key Responsibilities: 

Define and maintain the Domain Charter, including scope, interfaces and contracts, KPIs, SLAs, compliance controls, data ownership and quality standards

Lead end-to-end ownership of outcomes, including long-term strategic roadmap, delivery, QA, release planning, incident leadership and continuous improvement

Manage cross-domain dependencies, partnering with Origination, Credit and other teams to sequence work, negotiate trade-offs, coordinate cutovers and ensure backward-compatible interface versioning

Lead strategic platform initiatives such as ledger capability rollouts, reporting services or platform rebuilds, from business case through to delivery

Oversee regulatory and partner reporting, ensuring timeliness, accuracy and robust error handling across reporting obligations.

Support warehouse and servicing operations, including BAU reporting, trustee or treasury deliverables, and platform readiness for warehouse lifecycle activities

Maintain and communicate the platform roadmap, ensuring stakeholder alignment while identifying bottlenecks, technical debt and redesign opportunities

Partner closely with architects and engineering leadership to shape a cohesive platform strategy and delivery approach

Provide clear stakeholder and executive communication on progress, risks and key decisions, and facilitate governance and design forums

Act as a leader within the product and engineering teams by driving continuous improvement in product management practices and mentoring junior team members

About You: 

7+ years of product management experience, including significant experience in platforms or core financial systems

Experience in two or more of the following areas: core banking or ledger platforms, payments and reconciliations, securitisation or warehouse platforms, or financial/regulatory reporting

Proven experience owning finance or post-origination platform domains end-to-end, including incident leadership and control outcomes

Experience leading major platform rebuilds or foundational service improvements across multiple teams

Strong systems thinking and technical fluency, with the ability to work closely with engineers on platform architecture, data flows, APIs and financial controls

Excellent stakeholder management, structured problem solving and executive communication skills

Experience with Australian lending and reporting frameworks (CCR or ATO) is desirable but not essential

Exposure to payment rails, securitisation, servicer reporting or data governance and control frameworks is desirable but not essential 

What's life like at Plenti? 

Working at Plenti means joining a team that is high-performing, diverse, customer-focused and collaborative. It’s a place where everyone can “make it happen” and share in the results and success. If being in a dynamic industry as part of a market-leading fintech sounds like you, then join to help us reshape Australia’s financial eco-system for the good.

In return, you will enjoy a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship to take your career further. We offer flexible working and volunteer and study leave to develop your skills. We also have monthly social events, office snacks, company Townhalls and D&I initiatives and events to help you connect with your colleagues. 

Plenti is committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce. 

We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people with disabilities. When you apply, let us know of any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process so we can accommodate you.

The Opportunity - M&A Analytics Manager/Senior Manager

Key Responsibilities - M&A Analytics Manager/Senior Manager


Lead and manage analytics projects, ensuring timely delivery of high-quality insights to clients

Collaborate with clients to understand business needs and develop tailored analytics solutions

Analyse large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies that inform strategic decision-making

Present findings and recommendations to stakeholders, translating complex data into actionable insights

Support senior leadership with business development activities, including contributing to proposals, pitch materials, and client presentations

Mentor and develop junior team members, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement

Requirements

Maven Partners

At Fusion5, we're more than a solutions provider - we're a Transformation Partner. Across Australia and New Zealand, we help ambitious organisations go beyond by unlocking greater value, innovation and impact from their technology investments.
 
Across business applications, AI, cloud, data, integration and managed services, we bring together connected solutions that solve real business problems and deliver lasting value. We're deeply committed to our customers' success, and we bring that same energy and care to supporting our people.
 
We're now looking for a Practice Lead - Transformation & Strategy to establish and grow a market-leading, AI-first strategy and advisory practice in Australia. This is a rare opportunity to build something meaningful from the ground up, with the backing of an established, respected brand across Australia and New Zealand.
 
What You'll Do
In this role, you'll lead the creation and growth of Fusion5's Transformation & Strategy practice - scaling it from a founding team into a commercially strong, differentiated advisory business serving the upper mid-market. You'll position Fusion5 as the go-to partner for AI-led transformation, combining deep advisory capability with practical execution and a strong client-first consulting culture. Expect to:

Own the strategy, GTM & build of Fusion5's Transformation line of business in Australia, driving profitable growth, pipeline and utilisation

Build compelling business cases and AI-led roadmaps that move clients from ambition to action

Experience operating in strategy consulting or Big 4-style environments, with a strong advisory mindset and confidence engaging senior stakeholders on complex programmes
Demonstrated success delivering executive‑level strategy and transformation engagements

Credibility and presence when working with Board and C‑suite stakeholders

The ability to translate technology and AI into clear business outcomes and measurable ROI

What's in it for you?
"Go Beyond" isn't a marketing line at Fusion5, it's the standard we hold ourselves to. It reflects a convergence of solutions, services, and capabilities that enables clients to realise their business vision, with AI as a strategic lever for transformation.

We're proud to be a diverse, inclusive and forward‑thinking employer, and we're committed to creating a workplace where our people feel engaged, challenged, supported and successful. Alongside this, we offer a strong set of values and benefits that reflect how we work and what we stand for.

Collaborative, supportive culture where people enjoy working together

The Pepperstone story started in 2010. We know what it’s like to trade the world’s markets. Our team describes us as a place for the curious and the driven, and we like to do things a little differently; as a transformative global fintech we’re digital, nimble, connected, and united in our vision to create a better way to trade.

We thrive on progress – for our clients and for ourselves. Our organisational culture is ever-evolving, vibrant, diverse, global and results focused. You’ll find our 550+ team currently across 11 locations and 9 time zones.

The Role

The Project Analyst supports the successful delivery of strategic enterprise initiatives at Pepperstone. This role ensures effective project governance, reporting, and coordination across a portfolio of high-impact programs. Working closely with the Program Director, this position plays a key role in enabling executive decision-making through timely insights, risk management, and delivery assurance.

Key Responsibilities

Project Coordination & Governance
Support the Program Director in managing Pepperstone’s strategic project portfolio.
Maintain and enhance project governance frameworks, templates, and reporting tools.
Coordinate project reviews, steering committees, and executive updates.
Project Oversight & Support
Monitor project health, timelines, risks, and dependencies across initiatives.
Provide hands-on support to project leads in planning, scheduling, and reporting.
Ensure alignment with strategic objectives and enterprise delivery standards.
Reporting & Insights
Prepare high-quality dashboards, reports, and presentations for executive audiences.
Track KPIs and delivery metrics to support performance monitoring and decision-making.
Identify and escalate risks, issues, and delivery challenges proactively.
Stakeholder Engagement
Build strong relationships with project managers, business leads, and senior stakeholders.
Facilitate collaboration across functions to resolve delivery roadblocks.
Act as a trusted advisor to project teams on PMO best practices.


The role reports to the Project Director, based in Melbourne, Australia.

Preferred location for this role is: Melbourne, Singapore or Shanghai.We support flexible working. This position can be performed from both a mix of at our office and from home. Your manager will share details and expectations of your team’s regular cadence of working locations.

This is a fixed-term contract with a duration of 12 months.

About you

Demonstrated experience working in a similar role. 
Strong customer focus, the ability to relate to and understand our clients and a passion for providing excellent customer service. 
Proven ability to effectively communicate both written and verbal.
Attention to detail with the ability to maintain quality even in busy times. 
Strong relationship building skills, both internally and externally. 
Ability to use good judgment, act and make decisions with integrity. 
Desire and passion for continuous learning, improvement, and innovation. 
Ability to effectively collaborate and work as part of a team. 
Ability to work in a fast-paced and ever-evolving environment where priorities can change quickly.
Proficiency in working with technology, including Microsoft applications, Google drive, Gmail, Slack, MT4, MT5.
Fluency in English
Ability to live the Pepperstone values 
Committed to ongoing learning and development

Competitive salary structure including company bonus scheme

10 days of Company paid sick leave annually

Employee Assistance Program & Wellbeing Initiatives

We’re a regulated online Forex and CFD trading platform. With the scale of a global fintech and the agility of a start-up, we arm our clients with everything they need to take on the global markets with confidence. You will be part of a wider passionate and friendly team, and whilst things may not always go to plan, we learn quickly and move forward with impact.
